frame.oecd_flows_role: Ortega-Peri push-driven OECD flows justify macro GDP scale-up without micro fiscal sign; employer recruitment (Winkelmann) splits high-skill complementarity from shortage substitution
frame.health_bridge_role: Annual MEPS/SIPP cells are payer-incidence inputs — not lifetime NPV until age-path + discount + elderly phase applied
frame.tension: Working-age foreign-born health spend ≈18% below US-born in bridge cells, but 65+ Medicaid reverses; naive Medicaid-drain story fails at arrival, may bind at retirement

staging_gaps: MEPS/SIPP CSV copies not yet on external/lifetime/derived/stage3_proto/ — numbers mined from sources/immigration-fiscal mirror (builder meta confirms same schema).
Winkelmann (2001) is European employer-demand survey — maps to high-skill/OECD channel, not US <HS Mexico surge cells.

parameter_claims.notes: +1000 PPP$ (2000) origin-destination income gap → ~10% bilateral flow increase. Gravity push side — not US fiscal ledger.
Each tighter entry reform ↓ immigration ~6% on average. Policy lever absent from static lifetime NPV tables.
Push factors explain 30-40% of destination-year migration variation — treated as exogenous to destination economy.
Immigration ↑ employment/hours 1:1; capital adjusts within 1yr (ΔK/K ≥ 1); ΔA/A ≈ 0. US inflow 0.3-0.4%/yr → GDP +0.3-0.4%/yr, flat wages. Macro scale ≠ NAS micro fiscal sign.
38.8% employ foreign HQEs; among them FHQE share ≈11.1% of HQ staff. Germany economy-wide ≈3.5% (=9.1% × 38.9%). UK highest incidence (49.7%), NL highest conditional share (16.7%).

parameter_claims.population: German firms with FHQE, subjective motives Table 6
parameter_claims.notes: 89.8% reject 'lower wage demands'; 46% strongly agree 'speak foreign languages'. Complementarity > shortage-substitution for recruited high-skill — separate bucket from <HS fiscal cells.
Foreign-born working-age mean totexp $4,626 vs US-born $7,973 (−42%). Medicaid component $623 vs $1,065. Weakens arrival-phase Medicaid-drain prior.

parameter_claims.population: SIPP cells 25-29, all education buckets, nativity 2 vs 1
parameter_claims.notes: Bridge collapses insurance mix to nativity×age expected spend; invariant to education within nativity at 25-29 (education enters federal proxy separately). expected_mean_totmcd23: $600 FB vs $734 US.

parameter_claims.population: MEPS 2023, ages 65+, medicare insurance cells, nativity 2 vs 1
parameter_claims.notes: Phase reversal: FB 65+ Medicaid $1,282 vs US $364 (+252%). Lifetime NPV must weight elderly phase — working-age gap alone understates fiscal health channel for long-horizon immigrants.

parameter_claims.population: 98 SIPP working-age profile cells → MEPS branches
parameter_claims.notes: 294 bridge rows, 98 expected rows, zero fallback/unmatched. Ready for scenario_ledger join but not yet discounted to 75yr NPV.

unnamed_assumptions_cross_cutting: OECD macro 1:1 GDP scale-up (Ortega-Peri IV) treated as equivalent to NAS individual NPV — different unit, horizon, and public-goods scope.
Employer-recruited high-skill complementarity (Winkelmann) applied to stock-average or Mexico <HS surge composition.
Annual MEPS payer incidence commensurate with lifetime fiscal NPV without age-path, survival, or Medicare eligibility timing.
Working-age lower foreign-born health spend extrapolated to full lifecycle — ignores 65+ Medicaid reversal in module.
SIPP→MEPS bridge education invariance within nativity treated as 'health costs don't vary by skill' rather than 'insurance mix not yet education-stratified'.
Push-driven OECD flows exogenous to US destination fiscal conditions — valid for macro IV, not for cell-level SIPP donor matching.

generators.name: Push-flow vs pull-fiscal attribution split
generators.prompt: For every lifetime fiscal claim citing 'immigration increases GDP/jobs': tag whether evidence is Ortega-Peri-style push-IV macro (scale, flat per-capita) or NAS/CBO micro ledger (education × transfers). Block synthesis that imports macro 1:1 employment response into individual NPV cells without capital-tax and public-goods grid.
generators.retrodiction: Would have separated Ortega-Peri Table 5 ΔL/L≈1 from NAS -$109k <HS before debate conflated 'immigrants grow GDP' with 'immigrants are net fiscal drains'.
generators.negative_space: Standing frame: macro OECD panel = US lifetime fiscal scalar.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: GDP scale-up implies per-capita fiscal surplus
Push-driven flows uncorrelated with US Medicaid/Medicare enrollment path
generators.theory_tested: Macro push-IV migration variance should not rank-order NAS npv_education_benchmarks individual_npv

generators.name: MEPS annual cell → lifetime health NPV harmonizer
generators.prompt: Given meps_health_cost_module_2023 age×nativity×insurance cells: (1) map SIPP arrival age to age-band path; (2) apply survival/ Medicare eligibility; (3) discount at NAS 3% real over 75yr; (4) split payer columns (MCR/MCD/PRV/SLF). Never cite expected_mean_totexp23 as lifetime cost without horizon tag.
generators.retrodiction: Would have flagged scenario-composition memo disconfirmation #2 (FB working-age lower) AND elderly Medicaid reversal (L-009) before treating $600/yr bridge as −$109k NPV health line.
generators.negative_space: Annual cross-section MEPS mean = lifetime immigrant health fiscal externality.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Constant annual health spend from arrival age 25
Medicare/Medicaid incidence static at 2023 payer shares
Private/self-pay not netted against tax base in fiscal ledger
generators.theory_tested: Discounted 75yr Medicaid NPV from bridge cells ranks opposite working-age annual cross-section for long-stay cohorts

generators.name: Employer-recruitment channel vs stock composition
generators.prompt: When Winkelmann-style employer demand shows complementarity (languages, know-how; not wage undercutting), require separate high-skill recruited bucket in scenario ledger — do not pool with ACS stock <HS Mexico weights. Cross-check: firms with ≥1000 employees +32pp FHQE probability vs economy-wide 3.5% FHQE share.
generators.retrodiction: Would have blocked mapping IZA employer survey (IT/R&D 50-68% firm incidence) onto NAS average-recent-immigrant +$259k without education×visa path.
generators.negative_space: All immigrants modeled as undifferentiated labor supply shock.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Employer recruitment motives represent marginal US immigrant cohort
High-skill complementarity fiscal externality equals average stock NPV
generators.theory_tested: Recruitment-intensive industries should map to positive federal proxy cells, not <HS NPV benchmarks

generators.name: Lifecycle Medicaid phase-shift audit
generators.prompt: Compare MEPS mean_totmcd23 by age_band for foreign_born vs us_born. Identify crossover age where fiscal sign flips. For lifetime NPV: stress-test with (a) working-age-only horizon to 65, (b) full 75yr with elderly weights, (c) uninsured→Medicaid transition scenarios. Flag any headline using only 25-44 cells.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced L-007 (−42% working-age totexp) AND L-009 (+252% elderly Medicaid) as mandatory paired disconfirmation before Medicaid-drain advocacy.
generators.negative_space: Health fiscal sign from single working-age snapshot.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Immigrants remain in public-only insurance mix at US rates
YRSINUS tenure codes ignored for Medicare eligibility timing
generators.theory_tested: Elderly Medicaid cells dominate lifetime health NPV gap despite lower working-age means

generators.name: OECD policy-shock × health-bridge scenario grid
generators.prompt: Cross Ortega-Peri entry-law elasticity (−6%/reform) with SIPP scenario_ledger health columns. For each policy counterfactual (tighter entry, income-gap shock): re-weight origin cells AND recompute expected_mean_totmcd23 — immigration policy and health incidence treated as joint state, not independent scalars.
generators.retrodiction: Would have connected OECD law-reform flow shifts to which SIPP nativity/education cells enter the ledger, instead of static 98-cell health attach.
generators.negative_space: Immigration policy affects counts only; payer incidence held fixed at 2023 MEPS.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Entry restrictions select same health/insurance profile as stock
European employer survey parameters transfer to US policy shocks
generators.theory_tested: Policy-tightening scenarios change cell weights more than per-cell expected_mean_totmcd23

theories_tested.theory: Working-age MEPS/SIPP expected health spend ranks foreign-born below US-born and predicts lower undiscounted 25-64 health ledger
theories_tested.prediction: For ages 25-64, foreign_born weighted mean totexp and expected_mean_totexp23 strictly < us_born across bridge and module tables
theories_tested.duckdb_test: SELECT nativity_group, SUM(person_weight_sum * mean_totexp23)/SUM(person_weight_sum) AS wavg_totexp FROM meps_health_cost_module_2023 WHERE age_band IN ('25-34','35-44','45-54','55-64') GROUP BY 1; SELECT sipp_nativity_code, AVG(expected_mean_totexp23) AS exp_totexp FROM sipp_meps_expected_health_cost_cells_2024 WHERE sipp_age_band LIKE '25-%' OR sipp_age_band LIKE '3%' GROUP BY 1;
theories_tested.falsifier: Foreign-born working-age wavg ≥ US-born in either table after nativity_code harmonization

theories_tested.theory: Lifetime 75yr health NPV at 3% real flips sign vs working-age annual gap when elderly Medicaid phase included
theories_tested.prediction: NPV_75yr(totmcd) foreign_born > US_born even though annual 25-64 totmcd FB < US; working-age-only NPV component FB < US
theories_tested.duckdb_test: WITH age_path AS (SELECT age_band, nativity_group, mean_totmcd23, person_weight_sum FROM meps_health_cost_module_2023), discounted AS (SELECT nativity_group, age_band, mean_totmcd23 * POW(1.03, - (CAST(SPLIT_PART(age_band,'-',1) AS INT) - 25)) AS disc_mcd FROM age_path WHERE age_band ~ '^[0-9]') SELECT nativity_group, SUM(disc_mcd) AS approx_lifetime_mcd_npv FROM discounted GROUP BY 1; -- compare to NAS 3% benchmark; elderly 65+ bands must flip ordering
theories_tested.falsifier: Full lifecycle discounted Medicaid NPV remains lower for foreign_born after joining 65+ cells (L-009 reversal absent in warehouse)

theories_tested.theory: SIPP→MEPS bridge expected health costs are sufficient at nativity×age for scenario ledger — education gradient in health must enter via separate insurance path, not bridge column
theories_tested.prediction: Within sipp_nativity_code and sipp_age_band, expected_mean_totexp23 has zero variance across sipp_education_bucket (bridge design); federal/education effects live in sipp_scenario_ledger federal columns only
theories_tested.duckdb_test: SELECT sipp_nativity_code, sipp_age_band, COUNT(DISTINCT expected_mean_totexp23) AS n_totexp, COUNT(DISTINCT expected_mean_totmcd23) AS n_mcd FROM sipp_meps_expected_health_cost_cells_2024 GROUP BY 1,2 HAVING n_totexp > 1 OR n_mcd > 1; -- expect empty; JOIN sipp_scenario_ledger_2024 ON keys and verify health columns constant within nativity×age
theories_tested.falsifier: Any nativity×age cell shows >1 distinct expected_mean_totexp23 across education buckets — bridge silently encodes skill gradient
